channel.title: Comments for page "ARBH-Class Debrief File"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "ARBH-Class Debrief File"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The tone struck me as too informal. If it was presented more explicitly as a "handbook" or "training" or "tip sheet" document with a few stub footnotes to more formal sources I think it would hit the tone just right. That said, the grammar also struck me as off in some places.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded:
Echoing Shaggy here.

The humor here works to your advantage and prevents what might otherwise be a dry document from dragging.

I'd love to see more scenarios done in this format.
Wonderfully candid. I enjoyed the more laid-back nature of this document, it's quite refreshing to see an XK portrayed through internal documentation in a less deathly-serious manner. Great work!

item.title: Understandable
item.content:encoded: Yeah, you can't have all the skips. I had a longer list before, actually, but cut it. I don't have any Series 3 skips there so I might just add that one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do like this sort of formatting. It might be even used for different k-class scenarios. But I was let down to not see SCP-2794 here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love bugs. This is a great in-universe page and perfect for future writers who may want to write scips capable of causing ARBH-Class scenarios. +1
